facts: At light speed, you'd reach Mars in just 3 minutes, covering 140 million miles
Traveling at the speed of the fastest spacecraft, Voyager 1, it would take 70,000 years to reach Mars, a distance of about 140 million miles, or roughly 650,000 NY to LA road trips
If you drove your car to Mars at 60 mph, it would take around 257,000 years, or long enough to circle the Earth about 1.2 million times, a distance equivalent to 4.8 million football fields lined up
idea.payoff: and we still can't reach most of the cosmos within a human lifetime
idea.title: Mars in 3 Minutes? 🚀
idea.description: Imagine witnessing the red planet up close, with its towering mountains and deep valleys 🌌. But just how long would it take to get to Mars, and what are the cosmic speed limits that govern our travels through space? 🤔 Watch to find out and discover the mind-blowing truth about interplanetary travel 👽

scenes.visual: View of Earth and Mars rotating slowly in dark space, with a subtle question mark appearing between them.

scenes.text: Light Speed: Mars in 3 MIN! (140M miles)
scenes.visual: A stylized beam of light quickly streaks from Earth to Mars. The text highlights '3 MIN' and '140M MILES'.

scenes.text: Voyager 1 Speed: 70,000 YEARS to Mars! (650,000 NY-LA trips)
scenes.visual: An icon of the Voyager 1 spacecraft slowly traces a very long, winding path from Earth towards Mars. Small icons of cars driving across a map appear for comparison.

scenes.text: Your Car (60 mph): 257,000 YEARS to Mars! (4.8M Football Fields)
scenes.visual: A small car icon on an impossibly long road that stretches into space towards Mars. Visual counters for years and football fields animate.

scenes.text: Most of the cosmos is BEYOND our reach. For now.
scenes.visual: A breathtaking, vast shot of a vibrant nebula and countless stars, slowly zooming out to emphasize the immense scale of the universe.
